lessonTitle: Providing Anti-Oppressive, Anti-Racist Healthcare in Sickle Cell Disease

items.html: Clearly define the concept of anti-racist healthcare within the context of sickle cell practice
Recognize existing power structures within healthcare systems and their potential impact on patients and practitioners
Discuss ways to promote equitable access to healthcare services for individuals with sickle cell disease, focusing on anti-racist policies and practices

data.html:
Let's now change gears and be concrete about how we can move towards Anti-Oppressive, Anti-Racist Healthcare practices.

There are 3 spheres that we need to consider:

When it comes to our bias in medicine and clinical teaching, we can minimize and replace negative stereotypes by actively countering them.

How do we do this on a practical level?

images.caption: Please click and zoom into the text. Photo credit: Amutah, et. al., 2021

data.html: In 2021, a NEJM paper entitled "Misrepresenting Race — The Role of Medical Schools in Propagating Physician Bias", Christina Amutah et al. discuss the misuse of race in medical school curricula and the harm that this causes by propagating physician bias. Their group also provided recommendations to improve the use of race in medical education and medical research.

slides.title: Anti-Racist Healthcare Education Needs Diverse Perspectives

images.caption: Interdisciplinary — Nursing, Social Work, Physiotherapy, History of Medicine, Mental Health Services
Physicians — Black, Indigenous, People of Colour and White allies

data.html: It is imperative as a clinician that you are able to identify your own biases and how they can or do impact care.

images.caption: Please click and zoom into the various sections. Photo credit: (www.surgeryredesign.com)

data.html:
What zone are you in as we move towards anti-racist healthcare and practices? We all have work to do, and the 1st step is being able to identify where you are in this journey.

What education do you need to help inform your own changes? What education do your learners need to better arm them to deliver anti-oppressive, anti-racist healthcare to all patients?

images.caption: Now, let's look at Representation and Leadership.

data.html: Why? Patients trust their providers and providers are listening to their patients.

images.caption: Research shows that racial concordance can improve communication, trust, and adherence to medical advice.
Can we say that if a Black patient has a Black doctor, you're going to have better health outcomes? Yes, we can!
Evidence shows Black doctors provide better care for Black patients, says Karey Sutton, PhD, scientific director of health equity research at the MedStar Health Research Institute in Maryland.
Let's move into the 3rd sphere, which is Healthcare Delivery.

data.html: Anti-Racist Healthcare Practice Needs to Target All Levels

images.caption: Explore the image: International · Regional, Provincial & National · HCP, Clinical Settings & Community Engagement · Patient & Family

data.bubble_text: Healthcare professionals responsibly use their expertise and influence to advance the health and well-being of individual patients, communities, and populations.

slides.title: Determine Whether the Following Statements Are True or False

data.question: Misuse of race in medical school curricula causes harm by propagating physician bias.
data.feedback_correct: True. When it comes to our bias in medicine and clinical teaching, we can minimize and replace negative stereotypes by actively countering them.

data.question: Unconscious bias and stereotypes do not affect clinical teaching, patient care and outcomes.
data.feedback_correct: False. Unconscious bias and stereotypes will negatively impact clinical teaching, patient care and outcomes.

data.question: The three spheres for anti-racist healthcare practices are: Education, HR recruitment practices, and Leadership.
data.feedback_correct: False. The three spheres for moving towards anti-racist, anti-oppressive healthcare practices are: Education, Representation & Leadership, & Healthcare Delivery.

slides.title: Match the Levels of the Anti-Racist Healthcare Practice with Their Descriptions

data.prompt: Draw a line between each pair
data.feedback_correct: The target of all levels of Anti-Racist Healthcare Practice are the following: 1. GLOBALIZATION ENVIRONMENT - International / 2. MACRO LEVEL - Regional, Provincial & National / 3. MESA LEVEL - HCP, Clinical Settings & Community Engagement / 4. MICRO LEVEL - Patient & Family

items.html: We have interconnected social identities, and some of these will afford us privilege while others will not.
Unconscious bias and stereotypes negatively impact clinical teaching, patient care and outcomes.
We should actively counter stereotypes through meaningful diversity training and by being "upstanders".
As an ally challenge the social hierarchies and existing inequities, to create a more equitable and just society.

questions.question: My knowledge about Anti-Oppressive, Anti-Racist Healthcare in Sickle Cell Disease on a scale of 1 to 10 is ___ .
My comfort level with Anti-Oppressive, Anti-Racist Healthcare on a scale of 1 to 10 is ___ .
My confidence level with Anti-Oppressive, Anti-Racist Healthcare for patients with SCD on a scale of 1 to 10 is ___ .

items.html: Amutah, C., Greenidge, K., Mante, A., Munyikwa, M., Surya, S., Higginbotham, E., Jones, D., Lavizzo-Mourey, R., Roberts, D., Tsai, J., & Aysola, J. (2021). Misrepresenting Race — The Role of Medical Schools in Propagating Physician Bias. New England Journal of Medicine, 384(9), 872-828.

candidates.suggestion: S12 Statement 3: source marks '✓ True' for the statement that 'The three spheres are Education, HR recruitment practices, and Leadership' — but the Answer Text says 'False. The three spheres are Education, Representation & Leadership, & Healthcare Delivery.' The source ✓/✗ markers contradict the Explanation. correct_answer is set to 'False' following the Explanation (which carries the educational truth), not the ✓ marker. Review source for authoring error.
candidates.reason: Source contradiction: ✓ marker on 'True' for a demonstrably incorrect statement; Explanation authoritatively says 'False'. Explanation overrides the marker per educational intent.
